items.title: Ukraine to provide drone protection support in Middle East at US request
items.standfirst: President Volodymyr Zelensky on Thursday said that Ukraine would provide assistance to the US and other allies in ​response to requests for support in dealing with Iran's Shahed drones currently being used in the Middle East conflict.
items.content: President Volodymyr Zelensky on Thursday said that Ukraine would provide assistance to the US and other allies in ​response to requests for support in dealing with Iran's Shahed drones currently being used in the Middle East conflict.

items.title: Ukraine to help US and its allies counter Iranian drones in Middle East, says Zelenskyy
items.standfirst: Ukrainian president orders equipment and expertise to be provided to US in return to diplomatic support against Russia, saying ‘we help to defend from war those who help us’The United States and its allies in the Middle East are seeking Ukraine’s expertise in countering Iran’s Shahed drones, president Volodymyr Zelenskyy has said.Various countries, including the US, have approached Ukraine for help in defending against the Iranian drones, Zelenskyy said late on Wednesday. He said he has spoken in recent days to the leaders of the United Arab Emirates, Qatar, Bahrain, Jordan and Kuwait about possible cooperation. Continue reading...

items.title: Iran retaliates after Israel strikes Beirut and Tehran as war enters Day 7
items.standfirst: Iran fired missiles toward Israel Friday, Israeli officials said, after Israel launched fresh strikes on Tehran and hit Beirut's southern suburbs overnight.
items.content: Iran fired missiles toward Israel Friday, Israeli officials said, after Israel launched fresh strikes on Tehran and hit Beirut's southern suburbs overnight.(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)

items.title: Israeli strikes on Lebanon: Tel Aviv seeks to 'eliminate Hezbollah'
items.standfirst: The Israeli military said on Friday it had begun fresh strikes against Iran‑backed Hezbollah in the southern suburbs of Beirut, a Hezbollah stronghold targeted after a mass evacuation order for local residents. “One of Israel’s goals is to allow residents of northern Israel along the Lebanese border to live in peace,” FRANCE 24’s Noga Tarnopolsky said, adding that Israel is also seeking to “eliminate Hezbollah” amid the militant group’s deep unpopularity among many Lebanese as the conflict escalates.
items.content: The Israeli military said on Friday it had begun fresh strikes against Iran‑backed Hezbollah in the southern suburbs of Beirut, a Hezbollah stronghold targeted after a mass evacuation order for local residents. “One of Israel’s goals is to allow residents of northern Israel along the Lebanese border to live in peace,” FRANCE 24’s Noga Tarnopolsky said, adding that Israel is also seeking to “eliminate Hezbollah” amid the militant group’s deep unpopularity among many Lebanese as the conflict escalates.

items.title: War in Middle East live: Israel orders evacuation of towns in Lebanon’s Bekaa region
items.standfirst: The Israeli military on Friday ordered the evacuation of four towns in Lebanon’s Bekaa Valley after carrying out fresh strikes Friday on the southern suburbs of Beirut. Israel’s attack on Lebanon comes as it continues to pummel Iran’s capital Tehran. Read our liveblog for the latest developments.
items.content: The Israeli military on Friday ordered the evacuation of four towns in Lebanon’s Bekaa Valley after carrying out fresh strikes Friday on the southern suburbs of Beirut. Israel’s attack on Lebanon comes as it continues to pummel Iran’s capital Tehran. Read our liveblog for the latest developments.

items.title: 'Operation Epstein Distraction': Sexual assault allegations against Trump emerge
items.standfirst: PRESS REVIEW – Friday, March 6: International papers discuss US President Donald Trump's "warrior transformation" and speculate about how the new war in the Middle East could benefit Russia. Also: the US Department of Justice publishes an interview that outlines sexual assault allegations against Trump. Finally, is the US president trying to distract attention from the Epstein files with the new war? This question has inspired quite a few cartoons.

clusters.title: US Congress rejects "war powers resolution" that would halt strikes in Iran
clusters.neutral_headline: US Congress Rejects War Powers Resolution

items.title: US Congress rejects "war powers resolution" that would halt strikes in Iran
items.standfirst: In the US, Congress's lower house has narrowly rejected a resolution to curb President Donald Trump's powers in the Iran war. It's the second vote in as many days, after the Senate defeated a similar measure. While Democrats argue this is a war and needs congressional approval, the Trump administration defends that it is a "limited operation".
items.content: In the US, Congress's lower house has narrowly rejected a resolution to curb President Donald Trump's powers in the Iran war. It's the second vote in as many days, after the Senate defeated a similar measure. While Democrats argue this is a war and needs congressional approval, the Trump administration defends that it is a "limited operation".

items.standfirst: The US House of Representatives on Thursday narrowly rejected a war powers resolution seeking to halt President Donald Trump’s military strikes on Iran. The measure followed a similar Senate defeat a day earlier, highlighting divisions in Congress over Trump’s decision to bypass lawmakers and escalate the conflict.

items.title: US and Venezuela move to restore diplomatic ties two months after Maduro’s capture
items.standfirst: Re-establishing diplomatic relations will support Venezuela’s economy, US state department claims, amid push for minerals accessVenezuela and the US are restoring diplomatic ties, the two countries announced Thursday, in a new sign of thawing relations after Washington ousted former president Nicolás Maduro.The announcement came as US interior secretary Doug Burgum wrapped up a two-day trip to Venezuela, part of US president Donald Trump’s push for greater access to the country’s mineral wealth. Continue reading...

items.title: US, Venezuela restore diplomatic relations as Washington pushes for access to minerals
items.standfirst: Venezuela said Thursday it was committed to a “new stage” in relations with the United States "based on mutual respect" after both countries agreed to restore diplomatic ties. Acting President Delcy Rodriguez’s government said it was ready to pursue constructive dialogue grounded in sovereign equality and cooperation between the two nations.

clusters.title: Bernard LaFayette, Selma voting rights organizer, dies at 85
clusters.neutral_headline: Civil Rights Leader Bernard LaFayette Dies

items.title: Bernard LaFayette, Selma voting rights organizer, dies at 85
items.standfirst: Bernard LaFayette, the advance man who did the risky groundwork for the voter registration campaign in Selma, Alabama, that culminated in the passage of the Voting Rights Act of 1965, has died.
items.content: Bernard LaFayette, the advance man who did the risky groundwork for the voter registration campaign in Selma, Alabama, that culminated in the passage of the Voting Rights Act of 1965, has died.

items.title: Bernard LaFayette, civil rights leader who helped launch Voting Rights Act, dies aged 85
items.standfirst: Early in a life of service, LaFayette did the risky groundwork for the voter registration campaign in Selma, AlabamaBernard LaFayette, the advance man who did the risky groundwork for the voter registration campaign in Selma, Alabama, that culminated in the passage of the Voting Rights Act of 1965, has died.Bernard LaFayette III said his father died Thursday morning of a heart attack. He was 85. Continue reading...
items.content: Early in a life of service, LaFayette did the risky groundwork for the voter registration campaign in Selma, AlabamaBernard LaFayette, the advance man who did the risky groundwork for the voter registration campaign in Selma, Alabama, that culminated in the passage of the Voting Rights Act of 1965, has died.Bernard LaFayette III said his father died Thursday morning of a heart attack. He was 85. Continue reading...

LaFayette helped launch Voting Rights Act campaign
He did groundwork for voter registration in Selma
LaFayette's work culminated in passage of Voting Rights Act
He died of a heart attack on Thursday morning

clusters.title: Winter Paralympics opening ceremony clouded by boycott over Russia’s return

items.title: Winter Paralympics opening ceremony clouded by boycott over Russia’s return
items.standfirst: Controversy is overshadowing the Milan-Cortina Winter Paralympics, which kick off Friday with an opening ceremony boycotted by several countries protesting the decision to allow Russian and Belarusian athletes to compete under their national flags for the first time since 2014.
items.content: Controversy is overshadowing the Milan-Cortina Winter Paralympics, which kick off Friday with an opening ceremony boycotted by several countries protesting the decision to allow Russian and Belarusian athletes to compete under their national flags for the first time since 2014.

items.title: UK ministers will not attend Paralympics ceremonies because of Russian and Belarusian involvement
items.standfirst: No UK government ministers or officials will attend the Winter Paralympics opening and closing ceremonies after Russian and Belarusian athletes were invited to compete under their national flags.
items.content: No UK government ministers or officials will attend the Winter Paralympics opening and closing ceremonies after Russian and Belarusian athletes were invited to compete under their national flags.

ai_summary: Winter Paralympics opening ceremony boycotted by countries
Protest against Russian and Belarusian athletes competing
UK ministers will not attend ceremonies
Controversy overshadows Milan-Cortina Winter Paralympics
Russian and Belarusian athletes to compete under national flags

clusters.title: FBI Found ‘Suspicious’ Activity on Its Computer Networks
clusters.neutral_headline: FBI Finds Suspicious Activity On Computer Networks

items.title: FBI Found ‘Suspicious’ Activity on Its Computer Networks
items.standfirst: The FBI said Thursday that it had found “suspicious” activity on its computer systems, but didn’t provide further details.
items.content: The FBI said Thursday that it had found “suspicious” activity on its computer systems, but didn’t provide further details.

items.title: FBI confirms its networks were targeted by "suspicious" cyber activities
items.standfirst: The FBI said it "identified and addressed suspicious activities on FBI networks" and that it was responding but did not elaborate.
items.content: The FBI said it "identified and addressed suspicious activities on FBI networks" and that it was responding but did not elaborate.

ai_summary: FBI identifies suspicious activity on its networks
Activity found but details not provided
FBI responding to and addressing suspicious activities
No further information released by FBI
Incident raises concerns about cybersecurity threats

clusters.title: Oil price continues to rise amid Middle East crisis but stock markets rebound across Asia
clusters.neutral_headline: Oil prices rise amid Middle East crisis

items.title: Oil price continues to rise amid Middle East crisis but stock markets rebound across Asia
items.standfirst: Reports of attack on US registered tanker in Gulf lifts crude by 3% to $84 a barrel as gas price also starts to climbBusiness live – latest updatesMiddle East crisis – live updatesStock markets have rebounded in Asia after days of heavy losses driven by the war in the Middle East, but oil and gas prices have continued to climb amid disruption to supplies.South Korea’s KOSPI, which posted its biggest ever fall on Tuesday of 12%, rose by almost 10% on Thursday, while Japan’s Nikkei climbed by 1.9%. MSCI’s Asia-Pacific index excluding Japan jumped by 2.7%. Continue reading...
items.content: Reports of attack on US registered tanker in Gulf lifts crude by 3% to $84 a barrel as gas price also starts to climbBusiness live – latest updatesMiddle East crisis – live updatesStock markets have rebounded in Asia after days of heavy losses driven by the war in the Middle East, but oil and gas prices have continued to climb amid disruption to supplies.South Korea’s KOSPI, which posted its biggest ever fall on Tuesday of 12%, rose by almost 10% on Thursday, while Japan’s Nikkei climbed by 1.9%. MSCI’s Asia-Pacific index excluding Japan jumped by 2.7%. Continue reading...

items.title: European stock markets rally after report of ‘secret outreach’ by Iran to try to end war
items.standfirst: UK’s FTSE 100 up by more than 50 points, while pan-European Stoxx 600 share index rises 1.2%Business live – latest updatesEuropean stock markets have rallied on a report claiming Iran is engaging in a “secret outreach” to end the war in the Middle East, after several days of heavy losses on indices around the world.The New York Times reported that a day after the attacks began, operatives from Iran’s Ministry of Intelligence indirectly contacted the CIA with an offer to discuss terms for ending the conflict. Continue reading...
items.content: UK’s FTSE 100 up by more than 50 points, while pan-European Stoxx 600 share index rises 1.2%Business live – latest updatesEuropean stock markets have rallied on a report claiming Iran is engaging in a “secret outreach” to end the war in the Middle East, after several days of heavy losses on indices around the world.The New York Times reported that a day after the attacks began, operatives from Iran’s Ministry of Intelligence indirectly contacted the CIA with an offer to discuss terms for ending the conflict. Continue reading...

Gas price starts to climb due to supply disruption

Japan’s Nikkei climbs by 1.9%
